changeSummary: Added a small shared-runtime no-progress signal that classifies repeated same-tool completeness failures into contributor-agnostic structural buckets instead of relying on contributor names or tool allowlists.

Inserted a bounded stop gate in the completeness retry branch so a second same-class retry with no reduced missing-part count and no additional grounded tool success converges to an unresolved bounded answer instead of another same-tool self-heal.
Added focused heuristic tests covering the negative-control progress case and the repeated unresolved owner/entity stop case.

notes: The new stop logic lives entirely in `context/lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts` and keys off shared completeness signals, grounded tool success counts, and generic failure-text classes rather than contributor names, tool IDs, venues, or prompt-specific keywords.
No contributor `server.ts` files or route wrappers were touched; query/chat parity continues to flow through the shared `executeWithSelfHealing` engine.

The gate preserves the existing distinction between bounded unresolved execution and `needs_different_tools` capability misses by opting out when the completeness gap already looks unrecoverable or capability-constrained.
HTTP precheck against `http://localhost:3000` returned `200`, so the local app root was reachable during this run.

Public discovery precheck against `http://localhost:3000/api/v1/tools/search?q=Kalshi&mode=query&limit=3` returned `200` with `toolCount=3`, so the focus contributor was still discoverable on the local surface.

SDK query smokes could not be measured because the local `CONTEXT_API_KEY` source resolved to an invalid format (`invalid_context_api_key_format`) rather than a valid `sk_live_...` key; this is an environment/credential blocker, not a runtime-quality judgment.
Code-path parity still held because both `context/app/api/v1/query/route.ts` and `context/app/(chat)/api/chat/route.ts` import `executeWithSelfHealing` from `@/lib/ai/agentic-execution`.
No live browser chat QA was run; `chatPassed` reflects shared-runtime parity rather than a UI chat smoke test.

revertInstructions: If the orchestrator rejects this experiment, restore the files captured in `/Users/alex/Documents/context-sdk/examples/server/kalshi-contributor/validation/runtime-experiment-snapshot.latest.json` with `pnpm exec tsx "/Users/alex/Documents/context/scripts/runtime-research-experiment-state.ts" restore --workspace-root "/Users/alex/Documents" --snapshot "/Users/alex/Documents/context-sdk/examples/server/kalshi-contributor/validation/runtime-experiment-snapshot.latest.json"`.

After restoration, rerun `pnpm exec tsx --import ./tests/unit/server-only-shim.ts --test ./tests/unit/agentic-execution-heuristics.test.ts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx ultracite@latest check "lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts" "tests/unit/agentic-execution-heuristics.test.ts"`, and the SDK smoke checks against `http://localhost:3000` once a valid local `sk_live_...` key is available.

finalDecision.reason: Focused validation failed because repository-wide typecheck and lint gates were still red, and SDK query smokes remained blocked by invalid local credentials.

openRisks: Full-project typecheck is still blocked by pre-existing `context/lib/ai/scout-orchestration.ts` errors about `enableExactTargetExternalProbe`, so this experiment could not clear the required repository-wide type gate.
Targeted `ultracite` remains red because `context/lib/ai/agentic-execution.ts` already contains pre-existing formatting/performance/style debt outside this narrow experiment scope.
The local SDK query surface remains unmeasurable until `context-sdk/.env.local` provides a valid `sk_live_...` key, so the smoke gate could not confirm a non-error paid query response.

The new stop signal is intentionally conservative and only fires when the failure class is recognizable from shared completeness diagnostics; traces that describe the same no-progress pattern with materially different wording may still fall through.
